name: Shirley Horn
abstract: Shirley Valerie Horn (May 1, 1934 – October 20, 2005) was an American jazz singer and pianist. She collaborated with many jazz musicians including Miles Davis, Dizzy Gillespie, Toots Thielemans, Ron Carter, Carmen McRae, Wynton Marsalis and others.

about_quotes.speaker: Ron Carter
about_quotes.evidence: She had a band of me, Billy Hart and her. Did a lot of gigs together.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ron Carter — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Ernie Watts
about_quotes.evidence: All the things I do with Charlie Haden, I’ve done like five albums with Charlie Haden with the Quartet West. Quartet projects. String projects. Big string projects where we worked with Shirley Horn and Bill Henderson
about_quotes.doc_title: Ernie Watts interviewed by Monk Rowe, Toronto, Canada, January 11, 2003

about_quotes.speaker: Mandel Johnny
about_quotes.evidence: Shirley was as recently as 1990... We just happen to fortunately think quite alike when it comes to voicing but we disagreed quite a few times on chord choices... So she recorded as I recall what, three of your tunes
about_quotes.doc_title: Mandel Johnny — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Toots Thielemans
about_quotes.evidence: And Shirley Horn. She had the, she had the way and she, she only sounded good with these two guys... And I heard Shirley… I was at a session later, where she had top name fellas, you know.
about_quotes.doc_title: Toots Thielemans — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Wendy Oxenhorn
about_quotes.evidence: [Ken Kimery] Well, you’re in the archive, in the collections, with Abbey Lincoln, Quincy Jones, Jimmy Heath, with Shirley Horn, with Ella Fitzgerald...
about_quotes.doc_title: Wendy Oxenhorn — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Cedar Walton
about_quotes.evidence: Kimery: Billy Taylor, Shirley Horn, Elvin Jones, wow. ... [INTERVIEWEE] Oh, that's not you? ... [INTERVIEWER] Well, it sets the record straight.
about_quotes.doc_title: Cedar Walton — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
